content.problemStatement:
Write a program to print all the **leaders** in the array. An element is a leader if it is strictly greater than all the elements to its right side. And the rightmost element is always a leader.


Input Format

The first line contains NNN, the number of elements in an array.
The second line contains NNN integers, denoting the elements in the array.


Output Format
In a single line output all the leaders in the given array.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N, the size of the array. The second line contains N space‑separated integers representing the array elements.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing all leaders of the given array in the order they appear from left to right, separated by a single space.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Scanning from right, leaders are 2, 5, 17. Reversing gives left‑to‑right order 17 5 2.
All elements are equal; only the rightmost element qualifies as a leader.
Array is strictly increasing; only the last element has no greater element on its right.

solution.approach: A leader is an element that is strictly greater than every element to its right. Scan the array from right to left while keeping the maximum value seen so far (maxFromRight). The rightmost element is always a leader. Whenever the current element is greater than maxFromRight, it becomes a new leader and we update maxFromRight. Store leaders in a temporary list; they are collected in reverse order, so finally output them reversed to restore left‑to‑right order. This runs in O(N) time per test case with O(N) auxiliary space for the leaders list.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, where N is the size of the array.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for storing the leaders of a test case.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        n = int(next(it))
        arr = [int(next(it)) for _ in range(n)]
        leaders = []
        max_right = arr[-1]
        leaders.append(max_right)
        for x in reversed(arr[:-1]):
            if x > max_right:
                max_right = x
                leaders.append(x)
        out_lines.append(' '.join(map(str, reversed(leaders))))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
